{{Quote|Agent Ralls was such a clueless young tad that I thought I might have fun shocking him with tales of my travels with Papa Vader.|Davith Sconn|<ref name="Shield of Lies">''[[Shield of Lies]]''</ref>}}
'''Davith Sconn''' was an officer of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]], who rose to be first officer of a [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]], but was later confined at the [[Jagg Island Detention Center]] for his role in the [[Bombardment of Gra Ploven|bombardment]] of [[Gra Ploven]].

Sconn joined the Navy in around [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], probably at the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. He subsequently served for twenty-four [[Standard year/Legends|years]], spending almost all his time in [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. His longest continuous stretch on a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] was a forty-day period of compulsory liberty on [[Trif]].

While serving as commander of the second watch aboard the [[Fleet tender]] ''[[Moff Weblin]]'', he once visited the [[Black Fifteen]] shipyard at [[N'zoth]], in or before [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]]. While there, he heard stories about the bizarre honor culture of the native [[Yevetha]], and in particular the way that low-caste [[Gender/Legends|males]] were required to stop and offer themselves for impromptu execution whenever they encountered a higher-ranking individual. In a rare journey to the surface, he traveled to [[Giat Nor]] to see for himself if the stories were true.

Subsequently, [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] Sconn became executive officer of the Star Destroyer ''[[Forger]]'', under [[Grand Moff/Legends|Grand Moff]] [[Dureya]]. In around [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], however, Governor Dureya ordered him to carry out a punitive bombardment of Gra Ploven, in reprisal for the native [[Ploven]]'s refusal to make their assessed contributions to the Imperial war-effort. The ship's weapons were fired into the planet's [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]], creating boiling clouds that killed two hundred thousand Ploven in three coastal [[City/Legends|cities]].

In response to this atrocity, the Empire took punitive action, but it was Sconn, rather than Dureya, who was put on trial. His defense was based around the fact he had merely been obeying the Grand Moff's orders, but in spite of this, he was "forcibly retired" from the military and confined in the Jagg Island facility on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]].

He remained there through the planet's [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|liberation by the New Republic]], the [[Second Battle of Coruscant|Imperial reconquest]], and the subsequent [[Imperial Civil War|civil war]]. In [[16 ABY/Legends|16 ABY]], he made a deposition about his Imperial career to [[Agent/Legends|Agent]] [[Ralls]] of [[New Republic Intelligence Service|New Republic Intelligence]], prompting [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] [[Chief of State (New Republic)/Legends|Chief of State]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] interviewed him personally about his time on N'zoth.

In return for volunteering information on the Yevetha, Sconn asked to be allowed to take a short trip up into low [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]], to be in space one last time, a condition which Princess Leia managed to grant, and it was here that Sconn told her about his experiences on N'zoth.

==Behind the scenes==
The length of time Sconn had spent on Jagg Island in ''[[Shield of Lies]]'' shows that he had been convicted and incarcerated by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], not the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].
